content.thinking:
Let me analyze the three grids:

Target grid:
- Row 1: X X X X X (all filled)
- Row 2: X □ □ □ □ (first filled, rest empty)
- Row 3: X X X X X (all filled)
- Row 4: X □ □ □ □ (first filled, rest empty)
- Row 5: X X X X X (all filled)

Pattern: Alternating rows - odd rows (1,3,5) are fully filled, even rows (2,4) have only the first column filled.

Distractor 1:
- Row 1: X X X X X (all filled)
- Row 2: X □ X □ X (columns 1,3,5 filled)
- Row 3: X X X X X (all filled)
- Row 4: X □ X □ X (columns 1,3,5 filled)
- Row 5: X X X X X (all filled)

Pattern: Alternating rows - odd rows fully filled, even rows have alternating filled/empty pattern.

Distractor 2:
- Row 1: X X X X X (all filled)
- Row 2: X □ □ □ □ (first filled, rest empty)
- Row 3: X □ □ □ □ (first filled, rest empty)
- Row 4: X □ □ □ □ (first filled, rest empty)
- Row 5: X X X X X (all filled)

Pattern: Top and bottom rows fully filled, middle rows have only first column filled.

Key differences:
- Target has 3 fully filled rows (rows 1, 3, 5) with horizontal stripes
- Distractor 1 has 3 fully filled rows but even rows have a different pattern (checkerboard in columns)
- Distractor 2 has only 2 fully filled rows (rows 1 and 5)


I see a distinctive visual rhythm: three solid horizontal bands interspersed with single-column vertical lines. The grid creates a striking zebra-like pattern with clear, predictable alternation between completely filled and sparsely marked rows.

content.text: Expression: Three horizontal stripes with fully filled odd-numbered rows and even-numbered rows having only the leftmost cell filled

clem_player.response: Expression: Three horizontal stripes with fully filled odd-numbered rows and even-numbered rows having only the leftmost cell filled
